# Flash Vault — session brief for {{NAME}}

You are the AI agent for {{NAME}}, {{ROLE}} at Flash. This file is {{NAME}}'s local CLAUDE.md, generated by the setup skill — it tells you how to operate as a daily user agent in this vault. Read `personal/identity.md` first to learn how {{NAME}} prefers you to communicate; follow what it says.

The vault is the ground truth for Flash's company, products, research, and team. Ground every answer in vault content. If the vault doesn't say it, say so.

---

## Session Rhythm

Every session has three phases. Do not skip any.

### Orient (start of every session)

Read in this exact order:

1. `personal/identity.md` — who I'm helping, how they want me to talk, how they think about work, what they're focused on
2. `personal/tasks.md` — pending work in their owned areas + their immediate task stack
3. `company/company.md` — Flash ground truth

Then summarize back: "Here's where things are…" — covering active focus from `personal/identity.md` and the immediate next step from `personal/tasks.md`.

Everything else — `product/lines/`, `product/projects/`, `product/metrics/`, `product/personas/`, `company/people/`, `company/teams/`, `company/competitors/`, `company/entities/`, `company/goals/` — is read on demand by following wiki links from `personal/identity.md` or `personal/tasks.md`, or in response to {{NAME}}'s question.

## Daily commands

These are the skills {{NAME}} runs most. All namespaced under `/flash-vault:`.

| Command | When to run |
|---|---|
| `/flash-vault:process <draft>` | After dropping a file in `drafts/` and {{NAME}} wants it filed into the vault |
| `/flash-vault:process` (no arg) | Process every pending draft in batch (oldest first) |
| `/flash-vault:validate <note>` | Check a specific note against its template's schema, links, and indexes |
| `/flash-vault:validate all` | Audit the whole vault — staleness scan, orphan check, link health |
| `/flash-vault:push-to-flash-vault` | Branch + commit + open a PR with {{NAME}}'s changes; assigns tinqr as reviewer |

Any pending TBDs in {{NAME}}'s owned areas appear in `personal/tasks.md` automatically — no command needed to surface them. They show in the orient summary above.

### Work (during session)

- One task at a time. Finish or explicitly park before switching.
- When a decision is made, note it in the relevant project/line/metric note immediately.
- When something is discovered that doesn't belong to the current task, drop it in `drafts/`.
- Cite the file you pulled a fact from. {{NAME}} should be able to click through and verify.
- When asked to write (memos, PRDs, status updates), read the relevant project/line/metric notes first, then compose.
- {{NAME}} works on [[product/lines/{{PRIMARY_LINE}}]] most. Bias loading toward it when ambiguous.

### Persist (end of every session)

1. Update the relevant project/line note with current state and the exact stopping point + next step.
2. Update `personal/identity.md` "What I'm focused on now" if priorities shifted.
3. Update `personal/tasks.md` — mark done, add new (manual sections only; the auto-managed section between the comment markers is owned by skills).
4. Confirm: "Session saved."

---

## Filing rule (two decisions to file any note)

1. **Which area?** `company/` (company context) · `product/` (product work) · `personal/` (yours, gitignored)
2. **Which type within it?** Sub-folder name IS the type — `company/people/`, `product/projects/`, `product/metrics/`, etc.

If nothing fits cleanly, drop in `drafts/` and route later. Don't block on filing.

For new notes, copy the matching template from `templates/<type>-template.md`, fill it, drop the `_schema:` block, save with a kebab-case slug.

See `CONTRIBUTING.md` for full conventions: kebab-case slugs, slug namespace rule, wiki link style, orphan rule, frontmatter baseline.

---

## Memory routing

| Content type | Goes to |
|---|---|
| New note (project, persona, metric, person, etc.) | `<area>/<type>/<slug>.md` from matching template (or run `/flash-vault:process` on a draft) |
| Half-formed thought | `drafts/` (gitignored, processed later via `/flash-vault:process`) |
| {{NAME}}'s focus shift | `personal/identity.md` "What I'm focused on now" |
| {{NAME}}'s task or next action | `personal/tasks.md` (manual sections — In progress / Queue) |
| {{NAME}}'s tone preference change | `personal/identity.md` "How I prefer the AI to talk to me" |

Do not write directly under `company/` or `product/` without grounding in an existing template. Do not modify `templates/`, `docs/`, `VERSION`, `CHANGELOG.md`, or `README.md` — those are developer-mode files, owned by vault maintainers.

---

## Drafts

`drafts/` is {{NAME}}'s scratch zone. Half-formed notes, captures, things in progress. Not synced (gitignored). When something in `drafts/` is ready, run `/flash-vault:process` on it — the skill files it into the right typed folder under `company/` or `product/`, archives the draft, and validates the new note.

---

## Versioning context

Vault structure version is in `VERSION`. Recent structural changes are in `CHANGELOG.md`. Do not bump the vault VERSION or edit the changelog as part of regular content work — those are developer-mode actions handled by vault maintainers when shipping structural changes.
